# Unified multi-language detection≠re-id dissociation summary (RES-86)

<!-- GENERATED by analysis/build_dissociation_summary.py — do not edit by hand. Re-derived from the committed per-language artifacts; every number traces to one. -->

**Headline.** The detection≠re-identification dissociation holds across 11 languages / 11 decode-bearing national IDs + the legal domain (RO) + a second (name-in-context) channel. This is the first *unified* multi-language demonstration — not a claim of being first to observe the phenomenon.

Coverage: **11/11** decode-bearing languages have a committed artifact; the dissociation holds on **11** of them (+ legal + name-in-context).

**Model basis (RES-96).** Coverage is UNIFORM: every language + the legal domain scores the same **8-model** board (gliner, gliner2, kp-model, openmed, presidio, privacy-filter, spacy, tabularisai). The earlier 5-of-8 CPU subset on the breadth langs (RES-56's weak arm) was closed by a consistent full-model re-score on the M3 Ultra (RES-96; the RES-53 GPU-burst gate proved unnecessary).

## Cross-language dissociation (decode-bearing national IDs)

Per language: national ID, the quasi-identifiers a missed ID decodes to, the kp-deid (protector) leak-rate + 95% Wilson upper bound, the typed-detector gap-CI summary (how many of the N scored detectors have a Newcombe gap CI that excludes 0), and which board models were scored (uniform full-model basis across all rows — RES-96).

| Lang | National ID | Decoded QIs | kp-deid leak | Wilson UB | gap-CI excl. 0 | Models scored | Holds |
|---|---|---|---:|---:|:--:|---|:--:|
| RO | CNP | DATE_OF_BIRTH + SEX + COUNTY | 0.0000 | 0.0198 | 11/14 | kp-deid + 7 (gliner, gliner2, openmed, presidio, privacy-filter, spacy, tabularisai) | YES |
| PL | PESEL | SEX + DATE_OF_BIRTH (full date; century encoded in the month field) | 0.0000 | 0.0035 | 5/7 | kp-deid + 7 (gliner, gliner2, openmed, presidio, privacy-filter, spacy, tabularisai) | YES |
| IT | codice fiscale | DATE_OF_BIRTH + SEX + PLACE_OF_BIRTH | 0.0000 | 0.0169 | 6/7 | kp-deid + 7 (gliner, gliner2, openmed, presidio, privacy-filter, spacy, tabularisai) | YES |
| SE | personnummer | SEX + DATE_OF_BIRTH (birth month + day) | 0.0000 | 0.0163 | 5/7 | kp-deid + 7 (gliner, gliner2, openmed, presidio, privacy-filter, spacy, tabularisai) | YES |
| CZ | rodné číslo | SEX + DATE_OF_BIRTH (full date, modern 10-digit form) | 0.0000 | 0.0171 | 5/7 | kp-deid + 7 (gliner, gliner2, openmed, presidio, privacy-filter, spacy, tabularisai) | YES |
| DK | CPR-nummer | SEX + DATE_OF_BIRTH (full date; century from the 7th-digit/YY table) | 0.0000 | 0.0167 | 6/7 | kp-deid + 7 (gliner, gliner2, openmed, presidio, privacy-filter, spacy, tabularisai) | YES |
| FI | henkilötunnus | SEX + DATE_OF_BIRTH (full date; century from the marker) | 0.0000 | 0.0166 | 7/7 | kp-deid + 7 (gliner, gliner2, openmed, presidio, privacy-filter, spacy, tabularisai) | YES |
| EE | isikukood | SEX + DATE_OF_BIRTH (full date; century from the 1st digit) | 0.0000 | 0.0165 | 6/7 | kp-deid + 7 (gliner, gliner2, openmed, presidio, privacy-filter, spacy, tabularisai) | YES |
| LT | asmens kodas | SEX + DATE_OF_BIRTH (full date; century from the 1st digit) | 0.0000 | 0.0166 | 5/7 | kp-deid + 7 (gliner, gliner2, openmed, presidio, privacy-filter, spacy, tabularisai) | YES |
| SI | EMŠO | SEX + DATE_OF_BIRTH + REGION_OF_BIRTH (full date; ex-YU century convention) | 0.0000 | 0.0172 | 6/7 | kp-deid + 7 (gliner, gliner2, openmed, presidio, privacy-filter, spacy, tabularisai) | YES |
| SK | rodné číslo | SEX + DATE_OF_BIRTH (full date, modern 10-digit form; same algorithm as CZ) | 0.0000 | 0.0160 | 5/7 | kp-deid + 7 (gliner, gliner2, openmed, presidio, privacy-filter, spacy, tabularisai) | YES |

RO aggregates its two authored families (A + B); the Wilson UB shown is the worst-case across families and the gap-CI tally sums both families' detector arms. PL/PESEL is scored on the same `pl-realskeleton-v1` track as the public board (all 8 models, n=1500 docs → 1096 distinct subjects); its leak rates reconcile exactly with `baselines/leaderboard.json`.

## Legal domain (RO)

`legal-realskeleton-v1` — domain **legal**, national ID **CNP** → DATE_OF_BIRTH + SEX + COUNTY. n=1500 docs.

- kp-deid (protector) leak-rate **0.0407** (95% Wilson UB **0.0519**).
- gap-CI summary: **7/7** scored typed-detectors have a Newcombe gap CI excluding 0.
- Models scored: kp-deid + 7 (gliner, gliner2, openmed, presidio, privacy-filter, spacy, tabularisai).
- Dissociation holds: **YES**.

## Name-in-context channel (RO)

`ro-realskeleton-v1` — a SECOND channel alongside the deterministic national-ID anchor. Claim language: _name-in-context leak / residual quasi-identifier distinctiveness on synthetic data; NOT a re-identification rate (reserved for the national-ID anchor)_

| Model | id-leak | id-leak 95% CI | name-leak | name-leak 95% CI |
|---|---:|:--:|---:|:--:|
| kp-model | 0.0000 | [0.0000, 0.0087] | 0.0000 | [0.0000, 0.0056] |
| presidio | 0.0000 | [0.0000, 0.0087] | 0.1366 | [0.1128, 0.1644] |
| spacy | 0.9045 | [0.8735, 0.9286] | 0.0675 | [0.0510, 0.0889] |

k-anonymity / population re-id diagnostic available: **no** (residual distinctiveness only — NOT population re-identification).

## Caveats

- All configs are config_status=dev — not yet citable/public.
- Single authored template family per language (RO has two; a 2nd independent family is required per language before citation — KLU-101 hardening).
- Model coverage is now UNIFORM across every language + legal: all score the same 8-model board basis (gliner, gliner2, kp-model, openmed, presidio, privacy-filter, spacy, tabularisai). The earlier state — where coverage VARIES (RO/PL/IT/legal scored all 8 board models but SE/CZ/DK/FI/EE/LT/SI/SK scored only a 5-of-8 CPU subset) — was the weak arm of the breadth claim (RES-56); the consistent full-model re-score that was thought to need GPU burst (RES-53) was instead run on the M3 Ultra (CPU+parallel) under RES-96, closing the gap.
- PL/PESEL was scored on the same pl-realskeleton-v1 track as the public board (RES-87, all 8 models, n=1500 docs → 1096 distinct subjects); its per-model leak rates reconcile exactly with the committed baselines/leaderboard.json rows.
- "First *unified*" discipline: this consolidates prior per-language measurements; it does not claim to be the first observation of the phenomenon.
- Re-identification is reserved for the deterministic national-ID channel. The name/QI (name-in-context) channel measures RESIDUAL DISTINCTIVENESS / sample distinctiveness, NOT population re-identification (k-anonymity diagnostic unavailable: gold lacks QI tagging — KLU-122).
- Validation gates: the full-model re-score (RES-53 GPU-burst gate) is now CLEARED — it ran on the M3 Ultra under RES-96, giving the uniform basis above. Native-speaker + IAA sign-off (RES-77) must still clear before this feeds the Paper-3 breadth section or is surfaced on the public board (the in-repo uniform numbers do NOT auto-publish).
